title: Planning a Picnic
summary: Two friends, Maya and Jake, chat about planning a picnic in the park — they discuss what food to bring, which park to choose, and what time to meet.

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: Hey Jake, the weather looks great this weekend. We should go for a picnic!

body.speaker: Jake
body.text: That sounds like a great idea. Which park were you thinking of?

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: I was thinking Riverside Park. It has a lovely open area near the lake.

body.speaker: Jake
body.text: Oh yes, I love that spot. There's always a nice breeze there.

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: Exactly. So, what should we bring to eat?

body.speaker: Jake
body.text: I can make sandwiches. How about tuna or cheese and tomato?

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: Let's go with cheese and tomato. I'll bring some fruit — maybe grapes and strawberries.

body.speaker: Jake
body.text: Perfect. Should I bring something to drink as well?

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: Yes, please. Could you bring some lemonade? It would be really refreshing on a warm day.

body.speaker: Jake
body.text: Sure. I'll also pack a blanket so we can sit on the grass comfortably.

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: Good thinking. What time do you want to meet?

body.speaker: Jake
body.text: How about noon? That gives us enough time to get everything ready.

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: Noon works for me. Let's meet at the main entrance of the park.

body.speaker: Jake
body.text: Great. I'm really looking forward to it. It'll be nice to relax outdoors for a change.

body.speaker: Maya
body.text: Me too. I just hope it doesn't rain!

annotations.phrase: We should go for a picnic
annotations.meaningVi: Chúng ta nên đi dã ngoại
annotations.exampleEn: We should go for a picnic this Saturday.

annotations.phrase: Which park were you thinking of?
annotations.meaningVi: Bạn đang nghĩ đến công viên nào?
annotations.exampleEn: Which restaurant were you thinking of for dinner?

annotations.meaningVi: khu vực thoáng đãng, rộng rãi và đẹp
annotations.exampleEn: The garden has a lovely open area for children to play.

annotations.meaningVi: làn gió nhẹ, gió mát dễ chịu
annotations.exampleEn: Sit near the window — there's a nice breeze coming in.

annotations.meaningVi: Hãy chọn / quyết định chọn cái đó
annotations.exampleEn: There are two options — let's go with the cheaper one.
A cold shower is very refreshing after exercise.

annotations.meaningVi: mang theo một chiếc chăn / tấm trải
annotations.exampleEn: Don't forget to pack a blanket for the beach.

annotations.meaningVi: Nghĩ tốt đấy / Ý hay đấy
Điều đó cho chúng ta đủ thời gian
annotations.exampleEn: Leaving at eight gives us enough time to catch the train.
The holiday is next week — I'm really looking forward to it.

annotations.meaningVi: để thay đổi không khí, cho sự đổi mới
annotations.exampleEn: Let's cook at home for a change instead of going out.

annotations.phrase: I just hope it doesn't rain
annotations.meaningVi: Tôi chỉ mong là trời đừng mưa
annotations.exampleEn: I just hope it doesn't rain on our wedding day.

grammarNotes.title: Making suggestions with 'should' and 'How about'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'should' để đưa ra lời khuyên hoặc gợi ý: 'We should go for a picnic.' Dùng 'How about + V-ing' để đề xuất một ý tưởng: 'How about noon?' / 'How about tuna?' Cả hai đều lịch sự và phổ biến trong hội thoại hàng ngày.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'should' for advice or suggestions: 'We should bring a blanket.' Use 'How about + noun/gerund' to propose an idea: 'How about noon?' or 'How about making sandwiches?' Both are polite and common in everyday conversation.

grammarNotes.title: Polite requests with 'Could you'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Could you + verb' là cách lịch sự để yêu cầu ai đó làm gì. Lịch sự hơn 'Can you'. Ví dụ: 'Could you bring some lemonade?' — Bạn có thể mang một ít nước chanh không?
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Could you + verb' is a polite way to ask someone to do something, more formal than 'Can you'. Example: 'Could you bring some lemonade?' It shows respect and is standard in everyday requests.

translationVi:
Maya: Này Jake, thời tiết tuần này trông tuyệt đấy. Chúng ta nên đi dã ngoại!
Jake: Nghe hay đấy. Bạn đang nghĩ đến công viên nào vậy?
Maya: Tôi đang nghĩ đến Công viên Riverside. Có một khu vực thoáng đãng rất đẹp gần hồ.
Jake: Ồ đúng rồi, tôi thích chỗ đó lắm. Ở đó luôn có làn gió mát dễ chịu.
Maya: Đúng vậy. Vậy chúng ta nên mang gì để ăn?
Jake: Tôi có thể làm bánh mì sandwich. Bánh cá ngừ hay phô mai và cà chua nhé?
Maya: Thôi chọn phô mai và cà chua đi. Tôi sẽ mang hoa quả — có thể là nho và dâu tây.
Jake: Tuyệt. Tôi có nên mang đồ uống nữa không?
Maya: Có, làm ơn nhé. Bạn có thể mang một ít nước chanh không? Sẽ rất sảng khoái trong ngày nắng ấm.
Jake: Được thôi. Tôi cũng sẽ mang một tấm chăn để chúng ta có thể ngồi trên cỏ thoải mái.
Maya: Nghĩ tốt đấy. Bạn muốn gặp nhau lúc mấy giờ?
Jake: Trưa được không? Vậy là chúng ta có đủ thời gian để chuẩn bị mọi thứ.
Maya: Trưa thì được với tôi. Gặp nhau ở lối vào chính của công viên nhé.
Jake: Tốt lắm. Tôi thực sự mong đến hôm đó. Được thư giãn ngoài trời để thay đổi không khí thật tuyệt.
Maya: Tôi cũng vậy. Chỉ mong là trời đừng mưa thôi!

questions.prompt: Why does Maya suggest going for a picnic?
options: Because there is a festival in the park
Because she has too much food at home
questions.explanation: Maya says 'the weather looks great this weekend' as her reason.
questions.hint: Maya mentions the weather at the very start.

questions.prompt: Which park do they decide on?
questions.explanation: Maya suggests 'Riverside Park' and Jake agrees.
questions.hint: The park's name includes a reference to water.

questions.prompt: Why does Jake like Riverside Park?
options: It is close to his home
It always has a nice breeze
questions.explanation: Jake says 'There's always a nice breeze there.'
questions.hint: Jake mentions something pleasant about the air there.

questions.prompt: What kind of sandwiches does Jake offer to make?
questions.explanation: Jake offers 'tuna or cheese and tomato.'
questions.hint: Two options are given — one is a type of fish.

questions.prompt: Which sandwich filling do they choose?
questions.explanation: Maya says 'Let's go with cheese and tomato.'
questions.hint: Maya uses the phrase 'Let's go with...' to decide.

questions.prompt: What fruit does Maya plan to bring?
questions.explanation: Maya says 'I'll bring some fruit — maybe grapes and strawberries.'
questions.hint: Two fruits are mentioned — both are small and summer fruits.

questions.prompt: What drink does Maya ask Jake to bring?
questions.explanation: Maya asks 'Could you bring some lemonade?'
questions.hint: Maya says it would be 'refreshing on a warm day'.

questions.prompt: What does Jake say he will also pack?
questions.explanation: Jake says 'I'll also pack a blanket so we can sit on the grass comfortably.'
questions.hint: It is something to sit on when on the grass.

questions.prompt: What time do they agree to meet?
questions.explanation: Jake suggests 'How about noon?' and Maya agrees.

questions.prompt: Where do they decide to meet?
options: At the main entrance of the park
questions.explanation: Maya says 'Let's meet at the main entrance of the park.'
questions.hint: They choose a specific entry point to the park.

questions.prompt: What is Maya's final concern at the end of the conversation?
options: That they might forget the food
That the park might be closed
questions.explanation: Maya says 'I just hope it doesn't rain!'

blanks.explanation: 'picnic' — the main activity they are planning is a picnic in the park.
'park' — Jake asks which park Maya is thinking of.
'lake' — Riverside Park has a lovely open area near the lake.
'breeze' — a gentle wind; Jake likes the nice breeze at Riverside Park.
'sandwiches' — Jake offers to make sandwiches for the picnic.
'strawberries' — Maya plans to bring grapes and strawberries.
'lemonade' — Maya asks Jake to bring lemonade as it would be refreshing.
'blanket' — Jake says he will pack a blanket to sit on the grass comfortably.
'noon' — Jake suggests meeting at noon (12:00 midday).
'entrance' — they agree to meet at the main entrance of the park.
'outdoors' — Jake looks forward to relaxing outdoors for a change.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Maya and Jake spend time carefully planning their picnic together — what to eat, where to go, and when to meet. Do you think detailed planning makes an outing more enjoyable, or does too much planning remove the fun of spontaneity? What is the right balance?
